mnemonics.title: UDP Header Fields
mnemonics.content: SPDL - Source Port, Destination Port, Length (SPDL helps remember the 4 fields in order)

mnemonics.title: UDP Characteristics
mnemonics.content: CURE - Connectionless, Unreliable, Real-time, Efficient (CURE reminds you what UDP is good for)

mnemonics.title: UDP vs TCP
mnemonics.content: No FAN - No Flow control, No Acknowledgment, No Sequencing (FAN = Flow control, Acknowledgment, Numbering)

acronyms.usage: Core transport layer protocol for fast, unreliable data transmission
Uses UDP port 53 for name resolution queries
Uses UDP ports 67/68 for automatic IP configuration
Real-time voice communication using UDP for low latency
7-layer model where UDP operates at Layer 4 (Transport)
Maximum packet size; UDP datagrams may be fragmented if larger than MTU

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box divided into 4 equal parts (each 16 bits/2 bytes): Source Port | Destination Port | Length | Checksum. Total = 8 bytes = 64 bits.
Stack diagram: Application → UDP → IP → Network Interface. Show UDP as the bridge between application and network layers.
Side-by-side boxes: UDP = 8 bytes (minimal), TCP = 20+ bytes (with sequence numbers, acknowledgments, flags, window size).
Arrow from Sender App → UDP Header + Data → IP Packet → Network → Receiver UDP extracts data → Receiver App.

analogies.analogy: UDP is like sending a postcard - you write the address (ports), drop it, and hope it arrives. No delivery confirmation, no tracking, just best-effort.

If TCP is a formal phone call where you confirm the connection, say what you want, wait for acknowledgment, and repeat if needed, UDP is like shouting across a room - fast but may not be heard clearly.

analogies.analization: Think of IP address as the apartment building (building number), and UDP ports as individual apartment numbers within that building. Different applications live at different port numbers.

analogies.analogy: A news broadcast (UDP) sends out information continuously. If you miss part of it, you can't ask to replay - you just continue listening. Real-time but not guaranteed.
